description: Q-Learning task progress scorer. Measures how close the current page is to completing your task by computing cosine similarity between the task description and page content, then updating the Q-value for the current state.

parameters.task_prompt: string (required) — description of the task you are trying to complete
parameters.action_name: string (default: 'observe') — the action that led to this state
parameters.llm_score: float (optional) — your own confidence score between 0.0 and 1.0 for how relevant this page is

returns.state: current URL serving as the state identifier
returns.reward: combined score calculated as 0.7 * vector_similarity + 0.3 * llm_score
returns.q_value: updated Q-value for this state using Q(s,a) = Q(s,a) + alpha * (reward + gamma * max_future_Q - Q(s,a))
returns.visits: how many times this state (URL) has been visited
returns.vector_reward: raw cosine similarity between task tokens and page content tokens
returns.title: page title at time of scoring

when_to_use: After navigating to a new page, use this to check if you are getting closer to your goal. High reward (above 0.5) means you are on the right track. Low reward (below 0.2) means you should try a different path.
strategy_tips: Score after each navigation to track progress
If reward drops after a click, go back and try another path
Use explore_astar from the browser tool first, then score_progress to validate the choice
Compare q_values across different pages to find the most promising path
Provide your own llm_score when you have domain knowledge about the relevance of a page

learning_parameters.alpha: 0.5 — learning rate, controls how much new information overrides old Q-values
learning_parameters.gamma: 0.8 — discount factor, controls importance of future rewards vs immediate rewards
